title: Glimmer and Spark Make a Deal

pageTimings.text:
Glimmer and Spark made a deal.
Spark would teach Glimmer to breathe fire properly.
Glimmer would teach Spark to fly.
They shook on it.

On the first day, Spark explained fire.
He drew many diagrams.
The diagrams were very complicated.
Glimmer did not understand any of them.

Glimmer tried.
Smoke came out.
Just smoke.
"Interesting," said Spark, and wrote things down.

That afternoon, it was Glimmer's turn to teach.
Glimmer spread both wings.
Glimmer hovered for a moment.
Then landed.
"Like that," said Glimmer.

Spark tried.
Sparkles shot everywhere.
His hat fell off.
His feet stayed on the ground.
"I think I need more practice," said Spark.

On the third day, the diagrams had multiplied.
There were charts about heat.
Charts about breathing.
Charts about dragon anatomy.
Glimmer stared at them all.

"Spark," said Glimmer.
"You are trying too hard."
"Fire is not thinking."
"It is feeling."
Spark looked at Glimmer for a long time.

Spark put down his wand.
He put down his notes.
He stood very still.
He stopped thinking.
A small real flame came.

"What does flying feel like?" asked Spark.
Glimmer thought.
"Like falling upward," said Glimmer.
Spark nodded slowly.
"I think I understand."

Glimmer ran.
Glimmer spread both wings.
Glimmer jumped.
Glimmer glided five whole steps.
"That's more," said Spark.

At the end of the day, they sat down.
Spark could not fly.
Glimmer had not breathed fire properly yet.
But something was different.
Neither of them could say exactly what.

"Did we succeed?" asked Spark.
"I don't know," said Glimmer.
"Let's try again tomorrow."
"Yes," said Spark.
They both smiled.
